subject: Re: Jelinek - Minor Water Issues
body:
tommy will be very upset about the easement issues,,
and we will tell jelnik after closing that he flipped
the contract to someone else, can we close  earlier
than the 7th

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

> Gideon has advised me of the following issues which
> he says are not major  
> issues regarding Jelinek's water rights.
>  
> 1.    Jelinek has pending 2 water matters/cases
> which  we will have to see 
> through.  Approximate cost to do so for each case is
>  upwards of $12k per water 
> matter/case.  One matter involves complying  with
> periodic requirements to 
> demonstrate diligence in exploiting water  rights in
> order to keep thorse rights. 
>  Another case involves an  application to correct
> certain improper 
> descriptions with respect to grants  of certain
> water rights (Gideon will get me the 
> details -- but the Water  attorney assures me that
> the need to correct the 
> descriptions does not pose  a major water issue). 
>  
> 2.    Jelinek has several ponds on various areas of
> the  property as well as 
> the very large lake.  The rights with respect to the
>  ponds are junior rights, 
> so that during the July and August peak of dry
> season  when the water levels 
> in those ponds drop, we do not have rights to refill
> them  because the senior 
> right holders basically hold superior rights to use
> the  limited amount of the 
> water available at the time   
>  
> 3.    We need to get a new purchase price allocation
>  and once obtained need 
> to agree on it with Jelinek.  I have asked  Bart to
> get a new allocation to us 
> which I will forward to you and George for  comment.
>  
> 4.    As of 4:50 pm today, they still do not have 
> the property disclosure 
> statement from the Sellers.  I told Bart and  Glen
> to call Josh, David Kelly and 
> Jelinek until they get it.
>  
> 5.    Inconsistent legal descriptions have not  been
> resolved and will not 
> likely get resolved until early next week.
>  
> 6.    Title insurer has not yet agreed to insure 
> the two easements providing 
> access to each of the 1200 acres and 400  acres from
> the highways.  The 
> easement from the highway to the 1200 acre  parcel
> is particularly problematic 
> because it passes over the  properties of multiple
> owners and lacks a metes and 
> bounds description (so far  -- Gideon, Bart and Glen
> are still working on this). 
>  The easement to  the 400 acres is over a very small
> part of property owned 
> by one  owner, so it may not be that big an issue,
> but still they do not have a 
>  metes and bounds description yet. 
>  
> 7.    As of 4:50 PM today, our team has still not 
> received a copy of the BLM 
> Lease, though they have asked David Kelly for it 
> and David Kelly claims not 
> to have it.  I told Bart and Glen to  call Jelinek
> again.
>  
>  
>  
> One other issue that occurred to me when Gideon told
> me that they are  still 
> trying to find out whether or not they will be able
> to get the current  ranch 
> manager to stay on either permanently or at least
> for a short period while  
> our people learn about the ranch.
>  
> Problem is Ranch manager is probably close to
> Jelinek who will remain  in the 
> area anyway.  Ranch manager likely to find out who
> real owners are  and 
> likely to tell Jelinek.  Even if we do not retain
> ranch  manager, ranch hands will 
> likely talk and he will find out who the owners  are
> and tell Jelinek.  
>  
> How do you think we should deal with that issue.
>  
> I'll keep you updated as I learn more.
>  
> Darren  
>

body_html:
tommy will be very upset about the easement issues,,
and we will tell jelnik after closing that he flipped
the contract to someone else, can we close earlier
than the 7th

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

Gideon has advised me of the following issues which
he says are not major
issues regarding Jelinek's water rights.

  1. Jelinek has pending 2 water matters/cases

which we will have to see
through. Approximate cost to do so for each case is
upwards of $12k per water
matter/case. One matter involves complying with
periodic requirements to
demonstrate diligence in exploiting water rights in
order to keep thorse rights.
Another case involves an application to correct
certain improper
descriptions with respect to grants of certain
water rights (Gideon will get me the
details -- but the Water attorney assures me that
the need to correct the
descriptions does not pose a major water issue).

  1. Jelinek has several ponds on various areas of

the property as well as
the very large lake. The rights with respect to the
ponds are junior rights,
so that during the July and August peak of dry
season when the water levels
in those ponds drop, we do not have rights to refill
them because the senior
right holders basically hold superior rights to use
the limited amount of the
water available at the time

  1. We need to get a new purchase price allocation

and once obtained need
to agree on it with Jelinek. I have asked Bart to
get a new allocation to us
which I will forward to you and George for comment.

  1. As of 4:50 pm today, they still do not have

the property disclosure
statement from the Sellers. I told Bart and Glen
to call Josh, David Kelly and
Jelinek until they get it.

  1. Inconsistent legal descriptions have not been

resolved and will not
likely get resolved until early next week.

  1. Title insurer has not yet agreed to insure

the two easements providing
access to each of the 1200 acres and 400 acres from
the highways. The
easement from the highway to the 1200 acre parcel
is particularly problematic
because it passes over the properties of multiple
owners and lacks a metes and
bounds description (so far -- Gideon, Bart and Glen
are still working on this).
The easement to the 400 acres is over a very small
part of property owned
by one owner, so it may not be that big an issue,
but still they do not have a
metes and bounds description yet.

  1. As of 4:50 PM today, our team has still not

received a copy of the BLM
Lease, though they have asked David Kelly for it
and David Kelly claims not
to have it. I told Bart and Glen to call Jelinek
again.

One other issue that occurred to me when Gideon told
me that they are still
trying to find out whether or not they will be able
to get the current ranch
manager to stay on either permanently or at least
for a short period while
our people learn about the ranch.

Problem is Ranch manager is probably close to
Jelinek who will remain in the
area anyway. Ranch manager likely to find out who
real owners are and
likely to tell Jelinek. Even if we do not retain
ranch manager, ranch hands will
likely talk and he will find out who the owners are
and tell Jelinek.

How do you think we should deal with that issue.

I'll keep you updated as I learn more.

Darren

Darren K. Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
